text.title: pyproject-single-source Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for pyproject-single-source as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding a new tool's config to a fresh `.toolnamerc` file because "the README said to".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the tool reads `pyproject.toml` (it almost certainly does in 2026), use `[tool.<name>]`. The dotfile reintroduces the same drift problem the rule is preventing.

failure-mode.description: Configuring `ruff` in pyproject.toml AND keeping `.flake8` for "the parts ruff doesn't cover yet".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ruff covers all flake8 plugins as `--select` codes; if a rule is missing, request it upstream or use a single dedicated tool. Two parallel lint configs guarantee the agent edits one and breaks the other.
